evidence: Package decomposition (api, config, exploration, model, query, indexer, utils, viz) maps cleanly to domain capabilities; each package has a matching package-info.java that accurately states its purpose

API handler decomposition — SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers, ConcordanceHandlers, CorpusQueryHandlers, VisualizationHandlers — gives every domain slice a predictable, named home; a new engineer can immediately reason about which file to change for a given endpoint

GrammarConfig / GrammarConfigLoader / GrammarConfigSerializer trio follows a clean data-carrier / loader / serializer separation; callers that only need the data hold GrammarConfig, not the IO machinery
ExplorationService interface creates a well-named abstraction boundary between the HTTP layer and exploration domain; WordSketchApiServer depends on the interface, not the implementation

CLAUDE.md 'Code Structure' table lists 'query/QueryResults.java' but the file has moved to model/QueryResults.java — the canonical developer-facing document points a new engineer to the wrong directory

model/package-info.java Javadoc enumerates 'model.exploration.Edge' as an example pure DTO (line: '{@link pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model.exploration.Edge}') but no such class exists; the real Edge lives in viz.Edge — the package documentation is self-contradictory

viz package holds both a visualization utility (RadialPlot) and plain domain model types (Edge, RelationEdgeType); ExploreResponseAssembler in api imports from viz to build exploration responses, creating an api→viz dependency that is inconsistent with the api→model←exploration pattern visible elsewhere

strengths: Package-to-capability mapping is clean and intentional: each of api, config, exploration, model, query, indexer, utils, viz corresponds to a distinct domain concern; package-info.java files are present and accurate for most packages
API handler decomposition is exemplary: five named handler classes (Sketch, Exploration, Concordance, CorpusQuery, Visualization) give every endpoint family a stable, predictable home

Config triple pattern (GrammarConfig data carrier / GrammarConfigLoader IO / GrammarConfigSerializer output) is a textbook separation; callers that need only configuration data are cleanly insulated from file-IO and serialization concerns
ExplorationService interface provides a genuine abstraction boundary between the HTTP and exploration layers, with a documented rationale for why it bundles three exploration modes

model/package-info.java contains an unusually thorough explanation of its own design: it documents why some types are records, why ComparisonResult is a class, and why QueryResults was not split into a sub-package — this level of transparency is rare and valuable
high_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are not structural failures but documentation drift and one boundary ambiguity: canonical reference documents (CLAUDE.md and model/package-info.java) have diverged from the actual file layout, and the viz package mixes a plot utility with domain graph model types that the api layer needs to import.
high_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase architecture is solid and clearly intentional — packages align with domain capabilities, change boundaries are predictable, and the public surface (ExplorationService, GrammarConfig) is small and honest. What holds the score at 72 rather than 80-plus is the erosion of documentation trustworthiness: CLAUDE.md (the primary developer guide) points to a wrong file path for QueryResults, and model/package-info.java references a type (model.exploration.Edge) that does not exist, while the real Edge lives in viz. These divergences mean the 'project contracts and reference docs match runtime reality' criterion is only partly satisfied. The viz package boundary ambiguity is secondary but reinforces that the model-domain boundary has not been drawn with full deliberateness.

issues.summary: CLAUDE.md 'Code Structure' table lists query/QueryResults.java but the file lives at model/QueryResults.java
evidence: CLAUDE.md line 133: '│   └── QueryResults.java               # Result DTOs: WordSketchResult, ConcordanceResult' under the 'query/' heading
issues.suggestion: Update the code structure table in CLAUDE.md to show 'model/QueryResults.java' under the model/ subtree; also audit remaining query/ entries in the table for accuracy since the move likely happened as part of the model-package extraction

issues.summary: model/package-info.java Javadoc references pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model.exploration.Edge which does not exist
evidence: model/package-info.java lists '{@link pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.model.exploration.Edge}' as an example pure DTO in the model/exploration sub-package
No Edge.java exists under model/exploration/; the actual Edge record is at viz/Edge.java
ExploreResponseAssembler imports viz.Edge, confirming the real location
issues.suggestion: Remove the stale @link reference from model/package-info.java; if Edge semantically belongs in model/exploration (it represents a domain graph edge, not a visualization concern), move it there and update the viz package accordingly

issues.summary: viz package mixes a visualization utility (RadialPlot) with plain domain model types (Edge, RelationEdgeType) that the api layer must import
evidence: ExploreResponseAssembler (api package) imports viz.Edge and viz.RelationEdgeType to assemble exploration responses — these are plain domain graph types, not visualization-specific
Edge and RelationEdgeType are simple records/enums with no rendering logic; their placement in viz blurs ownership of the domain model
The expected dependency flow (api → model ← exploration) is broken by this api → viz cross-dependency for non-visualization data
issues.suggestion: Move Edge and RelationEdgeType into model/exploration/ (they are exploration domain graph types) and keep RadialPlot in viz; update ExploreResponseAssembler imports accordingly — this restores the clean api→model dependency without dragging in the visualization package

root_causes: A model-package extraction refactor was carried out (moving QueryResults from query/ to model/) but the canonical developer docs (CLAUDE.md, package-info.java) were not updated in the same commit, leaving them stale

The viz package was created to hold graph output types but the boundary between 'domain graph model' (Edge, RelationEdgeType) and 'visualization builder' (RadialPlot) was not drawn explicitly, causing domain types to accrete in a presentation-named package
